meta.purpose: LLM prompt to generate an Astro static page for Controller repair services

constraints.rendering: Static HTML only — zero client-side JavaScript unless explicitly marked as `client:load` island
constraints.styling: Tailwind CSS using semantic design tokens from the provided CSS variables. NEVER use raw color values (no `bg-blue-500`, no `text-white`). Use `bg-primary`, `text-foreground`, `bg-muted`, etc.
constraints.fonts: Inter via Google Fonts, loaded in <head> with `font-display: swap`
constraints.icons: Inline SVG only — no icon library runtime. Copy SVG paths from Lucide (https://lucide.dev). Each icon must have `aria-hidden="true"` and `focusable="false"`.
constraints.images: All `` tags MUST have `alt`, `width`, `height`, and `loading="lazy"` (except hero which uses `loading="eager"`). Use Astro `` component where possible.
constraints.links_internal: Standard `<a href="/path">` — NO client-side router. All internal links must start with `/`.
constraints.links_external: All external `<a>` tags MUST have `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"`.
constraints.accessibility: All interactive elements must be keyboard-accessible. Use semantic HTML (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<article>`, `<footer>`). Buttons must have visible focus rings.
constraints.accordions: Use native `<details>` + `<summary>` elements. NO JavaScript accordions.
forbidden: useState, useEffect, or any React hooks
Any content not present in this JSON

page.metaTitle: Controller Repair Winnipeg | Xbox & PS5 Controller Repair
page.metaDescription: Need controller repair in Winnipeg? We fix Xbox and PS5 controller issues including stick drift, broken buttons, charging ports, and connectivity problems.
page.heroDescription: We repair Xbox and PlayStation controllers in Winnipeg. Common controller problems include stick drift, buttons not responding, broken bumpers, or charging ports that stop working.

services.name: Stick Drift Repair
services.description: Replace worn analog stick modules to restore precise input.

services.name: Broken Bumper/Trigger
services.description: Repair broken bumper or trigger buttons.

services.name: Not Charging Fix
services.description: Controller not charging? We diagnose and fix charging issues.

services.name: Unresponsive Buttons
services.description: Fix buttons that don't respond or feel stuck.

services.name: Connection Issues
services.description: Fix controller connection and sync problems.

advantage_pillars.title: Save Money
advantage_pillars.description: Repairing a worn component costs a fraction of a new controller.

advantage_pillars.title: Professional Repair
advantage_pillars.description: Precise component-level work by experienced technicians.

pricing_micro_copy.costDriver: Controller repair costs depend on the issue—stick drift fixes are affordable, while Elite controller repairs may cost more due to premium components.
pricing_micro_copy.priceShock: Most controller repairs are surprisingly affordable compared to buying a new controller.
pricing_micro_copy.advisory: We'll explain costs clearly before starting any repair.
pricing_micro_copy.fallback: If repair doesn't make sense, we offer pre-owned controller swaps with trade-in credit.

faqs.question: Can you fix controller stick drift?
faqs.answer: Yes. Stick drift is one of our most common controller repairs.

faqs.question: Do you repair PS5 DualSense controllers?
faqs.answer: Yes. We repair DualSense and DualShock controllers.

faqs.question: How long does controller repair take?
faqs.answer: Most controller repairs can be completed the same day, often within an hour.

faqs.question: Is it worth repairing a controller?
faqs.answer: In most cases, yes. Replacing a worn analog stick or charging port is significantly cheaper than buying a new controller.

faqs.question: Do you repair Xbox Elite controllers?
faqs.answer: Yes. We service Xbox Elite Series 2 controllers.

service_area_note: Serving all Winnipeg neighborhoods. Walk-ins welcome or book online.

locations.name: St. Vital Centre
locations.address: 1225 St Mary's Rd, Winnipeg, MB R2M 5E5

stick_drift_visual.description: Stick drift happens when worn analog stick components send movement signals even when the joystick is not being touched.

validation_checklist: Page has exactly ONE <h1> tag matching the `page.h1` field
All services render with inline SVG icons, not an icon library import
All FAQ items use <details>/<summary>, not JS accordions
All external links have target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'
All three JSON-LD blocks are injected in <head>
No React, Preact, or JSX runtime in the build output
Hero image has loading='eager'; all other images have loading='lazy'
Page renders fully without JavaScript enabled
All color classes use Tailwind semantic tokens (bg-primary, text-foreground, etc.)
Phone links use tel: protocol; SMS links use sms: protocol
Section IDs match table_of_contents entries for anchor navigation
No placeholder content — every string comes from this JSON
